features.summary: Prologis reported strong Q3 2022 results with core FFO of $1.73 per share including $0.57 of net promote income. The company achieved record occupancy of 97.8%, same-store growth of 8.3% net effective, and rent change of 60%. The Duke Realty acquisition closed on October 3rd, adding $0.05 of accretion. Despite volatile macro conditions including inflation and rising rates, fundamentals remain strong with vacancy rates at historic lows and robust market rent growth of 28% in the U.S., up from initial guidance of 11%.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong fundamentals but is taking a cautious approach given macro uncertainty. They anticipate mid-to-high single digit rent growth in 2023 and are reducing development starts guidance to $4.2-4.6 billion with 60% build-to-suit focus. The company is temporarily pausing contributions to funds until there's better clarity on valuations, though this is voluntary rather than capacity-constrained. Management believes current market conditions remain at 9-9.5 out of 10 compared to the 12 level seen during COVID peak.
features.risks: Key risks include potential economic recession from aggressive Fed policy, rising construction and capital costs, and energy crisis impacts in Europe. The company faces redemption pressure in strategic capital with $1.3 billion in redemptions during the quarter. Currency volatility and geopolitical tensions from the war in Europe create additional headwinds. Some customers with mature supply chains have announced CapEx pauses, though management hasn't seen material impact on their portfolio yet.
features.key_drivers: Primary drivers include continued supply-demand imbalance with 22 months of supply and potential delivery gaps in late 2023/early 2024. The company benefits from 62% lease mark-to-market and 84% rent change on new signings. E-commerce demand is broadening beyond Amazon with 122 other customers driving activity. Supply chain resilience investments and inventory level adjustments support ongoing space demand, while reduced private developer competition due to limited leverage availability creates opportunities.
